description: Swarm state machine v2 - establish a single canonical snapshot, coordinator-only transitions, explicit verify and blocking rules, and cleanup-based rollout for reliable swarm execution.

userStories.title: Define canonical snapshot schema
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want one v2 snapshot schema for each swarm so that all authoritative state lives in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: Define one canonical file per swarm, such as `state.json`
Include `schema_version`, `rev`, `seq`, `swarm`, `workers`, `tasks`, `discussions`, `verify`, and `audit` in the schema
Require `schema_version=2` for all newly created v2 swarms
Document authoritative fields versus metadata fields
Document a minimal valid snapshot example
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Split swarm lifecycle from execution stage
userStories.description: As a coordinator implementer, I want `status` and `stage` to be separate so that terminal state and current execution work are never conflated.
acceptanceCriteria: Define `Swarm.status` as `active | paused | blocked | completed | failed | stopped`
Define `Swarm.stage` as `planning | dispatching | executing | discussing | verifying | repairing | idle`
Reject any state where `stage` uses terminal lifecycle values
Reject any state where `status` uses execution-phase values
Require `stage=idle` whenever `status` is `completed`, `failed`, or `stopped`
Persist `resume.stage` and restore it on `paused -> active`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce coordinator-only authoritative writes
userStories.description: As a system owner, I want only the coordinator to commit authoritative state so that writes remain consistent and auditable.
acceptanceCriteria: Allow only the coordinator to commit authoritative transitions to `state.json`
Treat worker, verify, discussion, and admin inputs as observations or commands until coordinator commit
Reject direct non-coordinator mutation of the snapshot
Record audit output for illegal direct snapshot mutations
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Validate swarm lifecycle and stage transitions
userStories.description: As a coordinator implementer, I want strict swarm transition guards so that lifecycle behavior stays predictable.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement the approved `Swarm.status` transition matrix
Implement the approved `Swarm.stage` transition matrix
Reject `completed -> active`, `failed -> active`, `paused -> completed`, and `blocked -> completed`
Reject blocked recovery when only elapsed time is present and no unblock evidence exists
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement worker timeout and unblock rules
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I want workers to move from waiting to blocked consistently so that stalled execution is visible and controlled.
acceptanceCriteria: Define `Worker.status` as `queued | starting | running | waiting | blocked | completed | failed | cancelled | stopped`
Move `waiting -> blocked` after the default `600s` timeout
Allow timeout override through `lark-opencode.json` key `swarm.wait_timeout_seconds`
Fall back to `600` when the config key is absent
Require explicit unblock evidence and coordinator approval for `blocked -> running`
Allow at most one non-terminal worker for the same `task_id`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Align task state with worker and verify outcomes
userStories.description: As a task orchestration engineer, I want task state transitions to reflect worker and verify outcomes so that dependencies stay correct.
acceptanceCriteria: Define `BoardTask.status` as `pending | ready | in_progress | verifying | completed | blocked | failed | cancelled`
Move a task to `ready` only when all dependencies are `completed`
Keep downstream tasks in `pending` when any dependency is `failed`, `cancelled`, or `blocked`
Do not auto-mark downstream tasks as `blocked` unless the coordinator explicitly writes that state and reason
Apply documented worker-to-task linkage for `in_progress`, `verifying`, `completed`, `blocked`, and `failed`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Model discussion rounds explicitly
userStories.description: As a discussion workflow engineer, I want discussion state and round handling to be explicit so that multi-round decisions remain deterministic.
acceptanceCriteria: Define `Discussion.status` as `idle | collecting | round_complete | consensus_ready | decided | exhausted | failed | cancelled`
Start `current_round` at `1`
Allow only the coordinator to trigger `advance_round`
Prevent new `collecting` rounds after `current_round == max_rounds`
Keep `decided` and `exhausted` mutually exclusive terminal states
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make verify state explicit and waiver-only when skipped
userStories.description: As a verification engineer, I want verify state to be explicit so that completion rules remain auditable and safe.
acceptanceCriteria: Define `Verify.status` as `idle | pending | running | passed | failed | repair_required | skipped | cancelled`
Allow `Verify.skipped` only when explicit waiver evidence exists
Reject any path that derives `Verify.skipped` from legacy data, inferred logic, or default behavior
Keep `swarm.status=active` and `swarm.stage=verifying` while verify is `pending` or `running`
Allow swarm completion only when all required tasks are `completed` and verify is `passed`, or `skipped` with explicit waiver evidence
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Commit snapshot updates atomically
userStories.description: As a storage engineer, I want each authoritative transition to commit atomically so that snapshot integrity survives process and delivery failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Allow only one coordinator writer per swarm at a time
Support multi-entity updates in one transaction over `swarm`, `workers`, `tasks`, `discussions`, `verify`, and `audit`
Commit through read current snapshot, build next snapshot, validate, write temp file, `fsync`, and `rename`
Record `reason`, `actor`, `at`, and `txn` for every successful commit
Generate a new `rev` and a strictly increasing `seq` on every successful commit
Treat failures before `rename` as uncommitted and failures after `rename` as committed-on-disk
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Publish authoritative snapshot and transition events
userStories.description: As a client integrator, I want a minimal authoritative SSE contract so that every consumer can rebuild current swarm state reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose authoritative SSE event types `snapshot` and `transition` only
Send a full `snapshot` when a client connects
Emit `transition` events after each successful transaction with monotonic `seq` values
Require clients to process events in `seq` order and de-duplicate repeated `seq` values
Require clients to request a fresh `snapshot` when a `seq` gap is detected
Send transition logs to the overall logging system without treating them as the source of truth
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Support consistent v2 reads across product surfaces
userStories.description: As an operator, I want Admin UI, Web UI, and TUI to read the same v2 state semantics so that status displays match backend truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Have Admin UI, Web UI, and TUI read from the v2 canonical snapshot or a read model derived from it
Render lifecycle state from `status`, not from derived guesses based on `stage`
Show `idle` stage for terminal swarms only when `status` is terminal
Keep archived swarms readable without changing lifecycle semantics
Display verify-skipped state only when waiver evidence exists in the underlying snapshot
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Separate archive from purge
userStories.description: As an administrator, I want archive and purge to stay distinct so that hiding data is never confused with deleting it.
acceptanceCriteria: Make `archive` change visibility only without changing `swarm.status` or `swarm.stage`
Make `unarchive` only clear `swarm.visibility.archived_at`
Do not introduce `deleted` as any lifecycle state
Keep auto-archive optional and default-off
Allow auto-archive only for `completed | failed | stopped`
Do not implement auto-purge
Allow purge only as a manual administrative action for `completed | failed | stopped` when no active worker, verify run, or discussion remains
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Roll out through cleanup and reset
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want v2 rollout to depend on cleanup instead of migration so that no mixed legacy state remains.
acceptanceCriteria: Do not implement legacy compatibility reads for v2 rollout
Do not implement legacy backfill for v2 rollout
Do not implement migration scripts that convert old swarm data into v2 state
Provide cleanup tooling with `dry-run`, explicit confirmation, deletion stats, deletion listing, and post-check validation
Block v2 rollout until cleanup completes successfully
Create all new swarms as `schema_version=2` after cleanup
Typecheck passes
